questions.prompt: Which of these is the correct authentication for the Huntress REST API?
choices: Basic Auth with Base64-encoded API Key and API Secret (e.g. hk_xxx:hs_yyy)
Username and password from the Huntress portal login
JWT bearer token issued by Entra ID
questions.explanation: The REST API uses Basic Auth with the API Key (starts hk_) and API Secret (starts hs_), Base64-encoded. Portal username/password is not accepted; the Key/Secret pair is the credential.

questions.prompt: The Huntress MCP server (api.huntress.io/v1/mcp) is read-only and uses the same API Key / Secret credentials.
questions.explanation: Per the MCP server documentation, all access is read-only and authentication uses the same API Key and API Secret as the REST API, either via OAuth flow or Base64-encoded Basic Auth header.

questions.prompt: A custom tool uses the Huntress Incident Report response API. Which design keeps the tool aligned with MSP runbook discipline?
choices: Auto-resolve incidents when the title matches a known false-positive phrase
Show the SOC remediation plan, require an authorised technician to approve, then call the supported endpoint and write the PSA note
Let the customer approve remediations directly from a public portal without MSP review
Use the API key in browser-side JavaScript so the helpdesk can act faster
questions.explanation: Write endpoints belong behind the same approval and evidence gates as portal actions. The tool can reduce clicks, but it should not remove authorisation, customer context, or PSA evidence.
